summary: First test pass for Adcom Media. Backend 6/6 pytest tests pass (root, contact create+persist, validation 422s, optional fields, _id not leaked). Frontend critical flows verified: header/hero with correct headline, 8 services cards in order, 5 case study cards, process 01-04 Discovery/Strategy/Creation/Growth all present, footer logo + 3 social links with valid hrefs, header CTA scrolls to contact, testimonial prev/next buttons clickable, contact form submit creates record and renders success message, mobile hamburger opens menu with all nav links visible.

minor.issue: On unexpected DB failure server returns 500 (caught and logged) - acceptable, but `logger` is referenced in except block while module-level logger is declared further down. It still works because module init completes before requests arrive, but ordering is fragile.

issues: Mobile menu nav buttons use prefixed testids `mobile-nav-*-link` (not the same as desktop testids). Works as designed; flagging in case automation expects unified testids.

action_items: Optional: move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` definition above route handlers in /app/backend/server.py (currently declared after routes — works but fragile).
Optional: align mobile nav testids with desktop testids (currently prefixed with `mobile-`).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger defined after route definitions; should be moved to top before routes that reference it.
server.py: contact list returns up to 500 docs without pagination — consider pagination for production.
Contact.jsx: budget chip buttons use unicode rupee char in data-testid (`budget-option-< ₹2L / mo`) — works but unusual; consider slugified testids for robustness.
All endpoints properly exclude MongoDB `_id` via projection — verified.
seed_data_creation: Test contacts created via POST /api/contact with TEST_ prefix names and example.com emails. Not auto-cleaned (no DELETE endpoint exists).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses pydantic EmailStr which rejects reserved TLDs like .test/.local — use @example.com in any new contact tests. Mobile nav links use `mobile-` prefixed testids. Section IDs: #work, #services, #case-studies, #about, #contact. Header.go() uses scrollIntoView smooth — allow ~1.5s for scroll to settle in assertions.
